name: Sendero de las Loberas (PRC-BU-69) — circular desde Caleruega
region_intro: Hiking around Caleruega offers a variety of trails through the Castilian landscape. The region features a mix of juniper forests, open plains, and historical sites, including remnants of Roman settlements. Elevation changes are generally gentle, with most routes involving moderate ascents and descents. The terrain is characterized by a blend of natural areas and cultural landmarks, providing diverse hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Caleruega The most popular hiking route is Sendero de las Loberas (PRC-BU-69) — circular desde Caleruega, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete, offering a circular path from Caleruega. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza (PR-BU-78) — circular desde Espinosa de Cervera, a moderate 9.2 miles (14.7 km) path, winding through the Sabinar del Arlanza juniper forest. Local hikers also love the Sabinar del Arlanza Trail (PR-BU 78), a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through juniper woodlands, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Caleruega is defined by juniper forests, historical sites, and open countryside. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Caleruega are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 180 hikers have used komoot to explore Caleruega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Caleruega?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Caleruega, offering a variety of experiences through the Castilian landscape. These include routes through juniper forests, open plains, and past historical sites.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Caleruega?
A: Yes, Caleruega offers 5 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners. These trails generally feature gentle elevation changes, making them accessible for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in Caleruega?
A: Many of the trails around Caleruega are circular, providing convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131478381/sendero-de-las-loberas-prc-bu-69-circular-desde-caleruega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de las Loberas (PRC-BU-69)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular path starting directly from Caleruega.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Caleruega is diverse, characterized by juniper forests, open countryside, and historical sites. You'll encounter a mix of natural paths and cultural landmarks, with generally gentle ascents and descents.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Caleruega suitable for families?
A: Yes, many trails are suitable for families, especially the easier routes with gentle gradients. The region's blend of natural beauty and historical interest can make for an engaging outing for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Caleruega?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Caleruega region. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to respect local regulations.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Caleruega?
A: Spring and autumn are often considered the best times to hike in Caleruega. The weather is typically mild, and the landscape is vibrant with spring blooms or rich autumn colors. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly, but hiking is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Peñas de Cervera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas. You can also find routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040352258/views-of-the-balsa-de-arauzo-de-salce-circular-from-arauzo-de-torre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Balsa de Arauzo de Salce&lt;/a&gt; specifically designed to showcase beautiful landscapes.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while hiking?
A: The Caleruega area is rich in history. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040359243/roman-town-of-clunia-circular-from-penalba-de-castro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman town of Clunia&lt;/a&gt;, an important archaeological site. The region also features remnants of Roman settlements and other cultural landmarks integrated into the hiking experience.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique natural features to see?
A: Absolutely. The area is home to unique natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3632504" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinarejos Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Yecla Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292500300/sendero-del-sabinar-del-arlanza-pr-bu-78-circular-desde-espinosa-de-cervera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Sabinar del Arlanza&lt;/a&gt; takes you through ancient juniper forests, which are a significant natural highlight.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Caleruega?
A: The trails in Caleruega are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers often praise the peaceful juniper woodlands, the historical points of interest, and the well-maintained paths that offer a diverse outdoor experience.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Many trailheads in and around Caleruega, especially those starting from villages, offer designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before you go.

Q: Can I reach the hiking areas by public transport?
A: While Caleruega itself is accessible by public transport, reaching specific remote trailheads might be challenging without a car. It's best to research local bus schedules to nearby towns and plan your route accordingly if relying on public transport.
tips: Caleruega is a small town that is located in a depression due to the small hills that surround it. In addition, it is bathed by the banks of the Río Gromejón.
The archaeological site of La Pudia shows the remains of a small early medieval village that developed between the 9th and 10th centuries.
Small pool of water from where you can observe a large number of birds and where, a few meters away, there is an observatory/lookout where a large number of vultures usually live.

Traditional tenada or shed built in stone and circular in shape known as loberas. They were used by shepherds to protect themselves when they accompanied their flocks through these fields.
Currently, they have been rehabilitated creating an ethnographic museum or interpretation center to maintain the history of this place.
The San Mamés site represents an ancient medieval hermitage known largely through archaeology. This was destroyed since its stones were used in other buildings.

waypoints.name: Tenadas de las Loberas Etnographic Museum
